texto: Societies in which women have lots of autonomy and authority tend to be decidedly male-friendly, relaxed, tolerant, and plenty sexy. Got that, fellas? If you're unhappy at the amount of sexual opportunity in your life, don't blame the women. Instead, make sure they have equal access to power, wealth and status. Then watch what happens.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Cacilda Jethá que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Societies in which women have lots of autonomy and authority tend to be decidedly male-friendly, …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amizade, equality, human-sexuality, com foco em termino e human-sexuality.

frase_original: Societies in which women have lots of autonomy and authority tend to be decidedly male-friendly, relaxed, tolerant, and plenty sexy. Got that, fellas? If you're unhappy at the amount of sexual opportunity in your life, don't blame the women. Instead, make sure they have equal access to power, wealth and status. Then watch what happens.

texto: Our sense of the full range of human nature, like our diet, has been steadily reduced. No matter how nourishing it might be, anything wild gets pulled - though as we'll see, some of the weeds growing in us have roots reaching deep into our shared past. Pull them if you want, but they'll just keep coming back again and again.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cacilda Jethá: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Our sense of the full range of human nature, like our diet, has been steadily reduced. No matter …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, human-nature, sexuality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de sexuality.

Pull them if you want, but they'll just keep coming back again and again.

texto: The conditions necessary for devastating epidemics or pandemics just didn't exist until the agricultural revolution. The claim that modern medicine and sanitation save us from infectious diseases that ravaged pre-agricultural people (something we hear often) is like arguing that seat belts and air bags protect us from car crashes that were fatal to our prehistoric ancestors.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cacilda Jethá: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The conditions necessary for devastating epidemics or pandemics just didn't exist until the agric…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, evolution, medicine pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de medicine.

texto: Anthropologist Donald Symons is as amazed as we are at frequent attempts to argue that monogamous gibbons could serve as viable models for human sexuality, writing, "Talk of why (or whether) humans pair bond like gibbons strikes me as belonging to the same realm of discourse as talk of why the sea is boiling hot and whether pigs have wings.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cacilda Jethá: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados.
